translated: Then the disciples of John came to him, saying, “Why do we and the Pharisees fast, but your disciples do not fast?”

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zibəənyɩ bɩ do nyɩntam mimbirenyɩntazaa, k'a lɛɛzaa b'a wusigə an bʊr har ʊ, an b'a ku an nɩ zibəə k'a ka ʊ bɩ barɛ.
translated: When a child is born, they are given a name so that they can be called, and if they die, that name is what they are remembered by.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bɩ, a muno wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Jɩm cɩna, k'ʊ dɩga a Elii zɛm b'a bʊmbɔ gɛ?»
translated: "He said to them, 'What are you discussing with each other as you walk along?'"

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Yirbəə wakatɩ soor k'a a ku bɩ, a a ya, an bɔ, an gʊɔɔ vantɔɔ yɩ ŋn'ɩ jɩnɩm daasɩyaa b'ʊ, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹Bɔ m, awɔɔ n gɔɔta naa ʊ dɔgta m saa…, b'awɔɔ ba zibəə nɩ y?›
translated: The woman said to her husband, “I am going, I am leaving, I will not stay in this house again,” and he asked her, “So, will you take the children with you…, or will you leave them behind?”

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Naa minto bɩ, k'a yɛ Wutuzaa bɩ han k'an zibəənyɩnɔ nyɔɔ a mɔsa zibəə bɩ tɔ ma.»
translated: If I had known, I would have told Wutuzaa not to let his daughter marry a stranger.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, awɔɔ ɩ dʊlɛɛrɔ bu hɔ ganwʊrɛ bɔŋ ʊ an n'a kam Woso ʊ, a bɔkarɛ, a hɔ k'an mɩŋŋa Woso lɛrɔ wɔɔ nɔ ʊ bɩ to, amba: Tɩrgabaa, mɩŋŋabaa, kan sɩrabarɛ kɩ, nawɔnnɔ awɔɔ a ga m, a ŋ ba, a ŋ gɔɔtarɔ wɔɔ ba sɔ.
translated: When the time came for him to be purified according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem to present him to the Lord (as it is written in the law of the Lord, “Every firstborn male shall be designated as holy to the Lord”), and they offered a sacrifice according to what is stated in the law of the Lord, “a pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons.”

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: Zebedee nyɩ Zakkɩ ta ʊ, kan a danzaa Zaan kɩ. Yeezuu tɔ vanta ka brɔɔ ma, a ʊ: Bʊanɛrgɛs, a lɛ taa k'a hɩ: bramahəənsire nyɩnɔ.
translated: Zebedee is the father of Zakkɩ, and his wife is Zaan. Jesus said to him: Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Yeezuu a nyɩ bɔ nʊ, a ʊ: «Ɩ jɛ bɔ mɔɔ jɩr ʊ, Sʊtaana! Bala, n y'a gʊrsɩra ba, n ʊ: Zuuba Woso y'a deem ɩbɩɩ nɩ yaa dam nɩ, ɩ da ʊ.»
translated: Jesus answered him, “It is as you say. But I tell you, from now on you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.”

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: Herod was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. He gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people, and asked them where the Christ was to be born. They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea; for so it is written by the prophet: ‘And you, O Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.’”

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: If you want to know me: I am not a child of the duure, I am not a child of the darɛ, I am not a child of the zarm either, but God made me and I am here! But who can say where I come from?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Naa kur bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ kɩ a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ lɛ taa k'ɩ hoser ba k'ʊ a yɩ.»
translated: Then the Pharisees went out and plotted how they might kill him, and Jesus said, "Pharisees, why do you want to kill me?"

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zibəənyɩ bɩ do nyɩntam mimbirenyɩntazaa, k'a lɛɛzaa b'a wusigə an bʊr har ʊ, an b'a ku an nɩ zibəə k'a ka ʊ bɩ barɛ.
translated: When the sun rises, it shines on everyone, whether they are good or bad, and it does not ask who is good or who is bad.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”

translated: Then, when we had gone to the farm, he said: "Jim Cina, did you see Eli's child at the river?"

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. translated: Herod and his people did this: they gathered together and planned to kill the child. They went to Bethlehem and searched for him, but they did not find him, because the child and his mother had already fled to another country. Then Herod became very angry, and he ordered that all the male children in that area be killed.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?

translated: If you want to know me: I am not a child, I am not an old man, I am not a woman either, but God made me a man who is not like me! But who can tell me who I am?

translated: When the sun rises and shines brightly, it helps people see clearly, and they know that it is day.

translated: Then, the man said to him, «Why did you let Eli’s mother come to you like that?»
